step_by_step_thinking: I agree with the majority opinion here. The maxillary artery plays a pivotal role by giving rise to various important branches including the deep auricular artery and others that form the pterygoid plexus, ensuring robust arterial supply to the infratemporal fossa. Additionally, although the external carotid artery has terminal branches involved, they don't serve as the primary main trunk supplying this specific anatomical region.
